rationale: This supervisor structure prioritizes safe, efficient exploration by preferring forward motion in clear conditions, using single-action commit states to avoid oscillations, and employing recovery/scan behaviors when obstacles are detected. Helper states (commit, scan, recover) ensure one-shot actions and clear transitions. Each state is total on uncontrollables, and only one controllable is enabled per state (except obs_front, which allows both move_backward and full_rotate for robust escape). The design avoids infinite loops and ensures systematic coverage.
strategy: Prefer forward motion in clear state for efficient coverage.
On front obstacle, escape by moving backward or performing a full scan (full_rotate) to find a new direction.
If obstacle is on the left, prefer rotating clockwise to avoid oscillation and vice versa for right.
Use commit states to ensure single, atomic actions and avoid repeated or oscillatory commands.
After recovery (move_backward), perform a scan to reorient before resuming exploration.
Always process uncontrollable events first to ensure safety and immediate reaction to obstacles.
Helper states (commit, scan, recover) ensure one-shot actions and prevent infinite controllable loops.
Transitions are deterministic and total on uncontrollables for robust, predictable behavior.
